title: agrostis stolonifera subsp. filifolia
intro: Agrostis stolonifera subsp. filifolia, also known as creeping bentgrass, is a subspecies of grass native to North America, characterized by its creeping stems and short, flat leaves. Traditionally, creeping bentgrass has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of creeping bentgrass include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of creeping bentgrass can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
